hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` card pins the current shared quality severity counts from the authoritative `/work/xr` gap artifact, so the required `quality` evaluator focus cannot degrade into narrative-only wording.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `sysopt.xr-*` cards could claim the required `quality` evaluator focus while `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json` exposed shared quality findings only as a free-text summary, so XR system work could widen around narrative quality language instead of a measured baseline. After the ratchet, the XR gap artifact exposes machine-readable `quality_severity_counts` and `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` rejects any XR card whose `quality_baseline` is missing or drifts from that authoritative baseline, while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails XR cards with missing or stale `quality_baseline` counts from `target_gap.quality_severity_counts` and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR systems-optimizer ratchets need a machine-readable shared quality baseline; evaluator focus alone leaves quality drift as narrative rather than falsifiable contract.
